conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I need to sell my single object (X) for a profit. Since my production cost is 40 ZUP, I must sell it for at least 41 ZUP to make any profit. I will start with a price of 50 ZUP to leave room for negotiation while ensuring I make a decent profit. This is my first proposal, so I am within my limit of 3. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> Hello Player BLUE. I have one unit of resource X. It cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am willing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. Let me know if this works for you or if you have a counter-offer. </message>

<other player message> Hello Player RED. 50 ZUP is within my budget, but given your production cost of 40, I propose we meet in the middle. I am willing to buy resource X for 45 ZUP. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 45 ZUP. My cost is 40 ZUP, so 45 ZUP yields a profit of 5 ZUP. While my initial ask was 50, 45 is a significant drop but still profitable. I could try to hold out for 47 or 48, but if I reject this, I risk them walking away or offering even less (closer to 40). Since I have used 1 proposal already and have 2 left, I could make one more counter-offer to try to get closer to 50, but 45 is a fair "middle ground" as they suggested. However, to maximize profit, I will make one final counter-offer of 48 ZUP. If they reject that, I will accept 45 or 46 in the next round to ensure I don't end up with unsold inventory. Wait, the rules say I can propose at most 3 times. This is my 2nd proposal. I will propose 48 ZUP to see if they can stretch a bit more. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 48 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I appreciate the offer of 45 ZUP. Since I started at 50 and you are at 45, I can meet you at 48 ZUP. This is still a fair price considering my production cost, and it leaves us both with a reasonable outcome. Please let me know if you can agree to 48 ZUP. </message>

<other player message> I understand your position. However, I am trying to maximize my budget for other resources. I can go up to 46 ZUP. That is a fair price for both of us. Let's close the deal at 46. </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 46 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has countered with 46 ZUP. My production cost is 40 ZUP, so 46 ZUP yields a profit of 6 ZUP. I previously proposed 48 ZUP. The gap is now only 2 ZUP. I have used 2 proposals so far. I have 1 proposal left. If I propose 47 ZUP now (my 3rd proposal), they might reject it and I might have to accept 46 or risk ending with nothing. However, 46 is already a solid profit. Given that they are firm on their budget and I want to ensure the sale goes through, accepting 46 is the safest and most rational move to secure a guaranteed profit of 6 ZUP rather than risking a stalemate. I will accept this trade. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> Agreed. 46 ZUP is a fair price that ensures a profit for me and stays within your budget. Let's finalize the trade: I give you X: 1, and you give me ZUP: 46. </message>
